title: Non-record: GQA + LZMA + Selective Pruning (val_bpb=1.1264)
author: ibarrajo
body:
## Summary

- **GQA (4 KV heads)** + LZMA compression + selective pruning + BigramHash 3072x112
- Adopts all safe innovations from merged SOTA #1019: fused Adam, ramfs data staging
- Score-first TTT (legal) improves from 1.1339 base to **1.1264 BPB**

## Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| val_bpb (base) | 1.1339 |
| val_bpb (TTT s_0) | **1.1264** |
| Artifact size | 14.1 MB |
| Step time | 95 ms |
| Total steps | 6,168 |
| Training time | ~585s |

## Key Findings

- GQA (4 KV heads) enables faster steps (95ms) but hurts TTT capacity
- TTT s_0 of 1.1264 is worse than Approach B's 1.1179 (full attention + TTT)
- LZMA compression yields better ratio than zlib, saving ~1.5MB artifact space
- Selective pruning only activates when artifact exceeds budget — clean design

## Why Non-record

GQA trades attention capacity for speed. The speed gain doesn't compensate for reduced TTT effectiveness compared to full-attention approaches (B6 achieves 1.1179).

## Rule Compliance

- [x] Training < 600s on 8xH100
- [x] Artifact < 16,000,000 bytes
- [x] No val tokens in artifact
- [x] GPTQ calibration within training budget
- [x] TTT is score-first only (legal)
